title: The 10 Best Natural Creams for Chapped Lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm and protect dry, irritated skin on the lips, promoting healing and relief from pain.
Flavonoids are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal dry, irritated skin, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with chapped lips.
Aloin is good for chapped lips because it acts as a natural emollient and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to soothe and heal dry, cracked skin, and protect it from further irritation.
Aloe-emodin is good for chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal dry, irritated skin on the lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a good choice for chapped lips because it helps to soothe and heal them. The cream is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which has been used for centuries to treat skin problems. When you apply aloe barbadensis cream to your lips, it helps to lock in moisture and protect them from the dry air. This is especially helpful during the winter months when the air is cold and dry. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling. As a result, your lips will feel softer, smoother, and more comfortable. Aloe barbadensis cream can be applied as often as needed, and it's gentle enough for even the most sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, you'll need dried Plantago lanceolata leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and vitamin E oil. First, infuse the dried Plantago lanceolata leaves in a carrier oil like coconut oil for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This will help extract the medicinal properties of the plant into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil and discard the leaves. Next, melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating. Once the wax and butter are melted, add the infused oil and vitamin E oil, and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Plantago lanceolata herbal cream is ready to use. Apply it to your chapped lips as needed to soothe and moisturize them.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for chapped lips because its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and heal dry, irritated skin on the lips.
Tannins are good for chapped lips because they help protect and soothe dry, irritated skin with their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, reducing swelling and promoting healing.
Saponins are good for chapped lips because they have soothing and moisturizing properties that help to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and protect the skin from further irritation.
Flavonoids are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin.
Kaempferol is good for chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe, heal, and protect dry, cracked skin on the lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a natural remedy for chapped lips. The cream is made from the petals of the marigold flower, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. When applied to dry, cracked lips, it helps to soothe and calm the skin. The cream's moisturizing properties lock in moisture, helping to repair and protect the skin. This is especially helpful for people with sensitive skin or allergies, as it's gentle and non-irritating. Calendula officinalis cream also promotes wound healing by stimulating collagen production and improving blood flow to the affected area. As a result, chapped lips start to heal faster and feel softer. The cream's natural ingredients also make it a good choice for people who want to avoid harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances found in some lip balms. Regular use can help to prevent dry, chapped lips from forming in the first place.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, first gather the necessary ingredients: Plantago lanceolata leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a small saucepan. Start by drying the Plantago lanceolata leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them, until they become brittle and crumbly. Then, grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add the ground Plantago lanceolata powder, vitamin E oil, and any other desired ingredients, such as honey or aloe vera gel, to the melted oil mixture. Stir well to combine, and then pour the mixture into small containers or jars. Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before applying it to chapped lips for soothing relief.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for chapped lips because they help to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and protect the skin from further damage, making them an effective natural remedy for dry, cracked lips.
Flavonoids are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin.
Allantoin is good for chapped lips because it helps lock in moisture, soothe and soften dry skin, and promote healing to repair damaged tissue and reduce inflammation.
Tannins help soothe and protect chapped lips by reducing inflammation and drying out, allowing the skin to heal quickly and reducing the risk of further irritation.

Polyphenols are good for chapped lips because they have powerful antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect dry, irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of new skin cells to heal and smooth out chapped lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale cream is a great treatment for chapped lips. It is made from the root of the comfrey plant, which has been used for centuries to heal skin problems. The cream contains compounds that help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further irritation. When you apply it to your chapped lips, the cream creates a barrier that keeps dry air from entering and causing more dryness. This helps to reduce the discomfort and pain of chapped lips. The cream also contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it feel smoother and more comfortable. Additionally, Symphytum officinale cream is easy to apply and can be used as often as needed to keep your lips hydrated and protected.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, first gather the necessary ingredients: dried Plantago lanceolata leaves, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and vitamin E oil. Start by making a herbal infusion by steeping one tablespoon of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves in one cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then letting it cool. Next, mix one tablespoon of coconut oil, one tablespoon of shea butter, and one tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan and heat it over low heat until the beeswax melts. Remove from heat and add the cooled herbal infusion, stirring well to combine. Finally, pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool and harden before applying it to chapped lips. The Plantago lanceolata in the cream helps to soothe and heal dry, cracked skin, while the coconut oil and shea butter moisturize and protect the lips.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for chapped lips because it helps to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and numb the pain, making it a soothing and protective treatment for dry and irritated lip skin.

Rosmarinic acid is good for chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe, calm, and protect dry, irritated skin, reducing redness and promoting healing.
Menthone is good for chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe and protect dry, cracked skin on the lips.
Quercetin is good for chapped lips because it has powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin.
Tannins are good for chapped lips because they help protect and soothe the skin with their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing redness and dryness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a helpful ingredient in lip creams. When applied to chapped lips, peppermint oil has a cooling effect that reduces swelling and pain. This is because peppermint contains a chemical called menthol, which helps to numb the skin and make it feel better. The cream also has moisturizing properties, thanks to ingredients like beeswax or coconut oil, that lock in moisture and protect the lips from further drying out. As a result, chapped lips feel soothed and begin to heal faster. Peppermint's antiseptic properties also help to prevent infections, which can be a problem with cracked and bleeding lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal creams that help with chapped lips, you will need to gather the necessary ingredients and equipment. First, dry the Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers completely, either by air-drying them or using a dehydrator. Once dry, grind the plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine the powder with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:5, for example, 1 part plant material to 5 parts oil. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until it reaches a temperature of around 150-180°F (65-82°C). Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before straining it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Allow the mixture to cool completely before whipping it with a hand mixer or whisk until it thickens and becomes creamy. Store the herbal cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator, where it will thicken further and be ready to use on chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin, allowing it to heal and feel smoother.

Flavonoids are good for chapped lips because they have strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin on the lips, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Saponins are good for chapped lips because they have natural moisturizing and soothing properties that help to repair and protect dry, cracked skin on the lips.
Polyphenols are good for chapped lips because they have strong antioxidant properties that help protect and soothe dry, irritated skin by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams are good for chapped lips because they help to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin. The active ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and redness, making it easier to heal. This cream also helps to lock in moisture, keeping the lips hydrated and soft. Glycyrrhiza glabra has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various skin problems, including chapped lips. It also contains antioxidants that protect the skin from environmental damage and help to repair damaged skin cells. When applied regularly, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to heal and prevent chapped lips, leaving them smooth, soft, and healthy-looking.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the plant. If using fresh leaves, dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Once the leaves are dry, crush them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix the powdered Plantago lanceolata with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of about 1:10, so if you use 1 tablespoon of the powder, add 10 tablespoons of the carrier oil. Then, heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating until the mixture reaches a temperature of about 160-180°F. Finally, add this infused oil to a base cream or moisturizer, like shea butter or beeswax, and mix well to create a smooth and soothing cream that can help heal and protect chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and heal dry, irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy lip tissue regeneration.
Quercetin is good for chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin, reducing swelling and discomfort.
Tannins are good for chapped lips because they help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, providing relief from dryness and irritation.
Polyphenols are good for chapped lips because they help to protect and soothe dry, irritated skin by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cells.
Terpinen-4-ol is good for chapped lips because it has strong healing properties that help to lock in moisture, soothe dryness, and reduce inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream is great for chapped lips because it has healing properties. The cream is made from tea tree oil, which comes from the leaves of the Melaleuca tree. Tea tree oil is a natural antibiotic that helps fight off the bacteria that can cause chapped lips. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling. The cream is rich in antioxidants, which protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. When applied to chapped lips, the cream helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin. This helps to repair the skin and reduce the appearance of chapped lips. The cream is also non-greasy and easily absorbed into the skin, making it a great option for people with dry or sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Plantago lanceolata leaves, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and a few drops of vitamin E oil. First, combine the dried leaves with a carrier oil like coconut oil in a small saucepan, and gently heat the mixture over low heat to release the plant's medicinal properties. Allow the mixture to steep for about 20-30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Next, in a separate saucepan, melt the shea butter and beeswax over low heat, stirring occasionally until they're fully incorporated. Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly add the strained plant infusion to the melted shea and beeswax mixture, stirring constantly to avoid any lumps. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened, then transfer it to a clean container and add a few drops of vitamin E oil. Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator to use as needed for chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm and heal dry, irritated skin on the lips.
Polyphenols are good for chapped lips because they have antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect dry, irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Flavonoids are good for chapped lips because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and heal dry, irritated skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams can help with chapped lips because they have soothing and moisturizing properties. The cream contains active compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that calm and protect the skin. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and redness, which can make chapped lips feel better. The cream also locks in moisture, which helps to repair and heal dry, cracked skin. This can lead to softer, smoother lips that are less prone to further irritation. When applied regularly, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide long-lasting relief from chapped, dry lips, making it easier to enjoy everyday activities without discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, start by gathering plantain leaves and flowers from a trusted source. Clean and dry the plant material thoroughly, then infuse it in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for several weeks. This process allows the plant's medicinal properties to transfer into the oil. Strain the oil and mix it with a thickening agent like beeswax, shea butter, or vitamin E oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl to combine the ingredients. Once melted, remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken. Transfer the mixture to a small tin or jar and let it cool completely before use. To apply the cream to chapped lips, gently massage a small amount onto the affected area, and enjoy the soothing benefits of Plantago lanceolata's natural moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for chapped lips because it helps to protect and soothe dry skin by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, reducing redness and promoting healthy cell growth.
Quercetin is good for chapped lips because it helps to soothe and protect the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent.
Tannins are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, cracked skin on the lips, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
Luteolin is good for chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect dry, cracked skin on the lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams are good for chapped lips because they contain the extract of the Melissa plant. This plant is known for its soothing and calming properties. When applied to dry and chapped lips, the Melissa extract helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain. It also has antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing. The cream also moisturizes the lips, keeping them soft and smooth. Additionally, Melissa officinalis creams are often fragrance-free and non-irritating, making them suitable for people with sensitive skin. They can be applied regularly to help repair and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal creams for chapped lips, start by gathering the right ingredients. You'll need dried Plantago lanceolata leaves, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and essential oils like peppermint or lavender. Begin by infusing the dried leaves in a carrier oil like coconut oil for several weeks to extract the herb's medicinal properties. Strain the mixture and combine it with shea butter and beeswax in a double boiler to melt and mix them. Once the mixture is smooth, remove it from the heat and let it cool until it thickens. Stir in a few drops of peppermint or lavender essential oil for fragrance and added benefits. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before applying it to chapped lips. Plantago lanceolata herbal creams work by soothing and moisturizing the skin, helping to heal and protect chapped lips from further irritation.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Fatty acids are good for chapped lips because they have moisturizing properties that help lock in moisture and soothe dry skin, providing relief from chapped and cracked lips.
Polyphenols are good for chapped lips because they have strong antioxidant properties that help repair and soothe dry, irritated skin, locking in moisture and reducing inflammation.
Vitamin E is good for chapped lips because it helps to protect and heal the skin by locking in moisture and reducing inflammation, which soothes and smooths out dry, cracked lip areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cocos nucifera, also known as coconut, is a natural ingredient that's good for chapped lips. The oil in coconut, called lauric acid, helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin. When you apply coconut oil to your lips, it creates a barrier that prevents dry air from entering and causing more irritation. This helps to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips. Coconut oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness. As the oil absorbs into the skin, it helps to promote the growth of new skin cells, leaving your lips feeling smooth and soft. Plus, coconut oil is non-toxic and hypoallergenic, making it a great option for people with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, first you need to dry the Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers. This can be done by tying the leaves and flowers in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once they are completely dry, you can make an infusion by steeping 2-3 tablespoons of dried leaves and flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let the infusion cool down, then strain it and discard the solids. Next, you need to make a cream base by mixing 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until it's fully melted and smooth. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes, then slowly add the cooled Plantago lanceolata infusion to the cream base, stirring constantly to prevent lumps. Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool completely before using it. This cream can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks and can be applied to chapped lips to help soothe and heal them.
remedies.image_alt: Cocos nucifera herbal creams for chapped lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for chapped lips because they help to protect the skin, reduce inflammation, and lock in moisture, making them a natural remedy for soothing and healing dry, cracked lips.
Polyphenols are good for chapped lips because they have strong antioxidant properties that help protect and heal damaged skin by reducing inflammation and promoting collagen production.
Flavonoids are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal dry, irritated skin on the lips.

Saponins are good for chapped lips because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe, moisturize, and protect dry, cracked skin on the lips, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Apoligenin is good for chapped lips because it helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin from dryness and irritation, making it a soothing and effective natural remedy for dry, cracked lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata creams are made from the leaves of the Plantago lanceolata plant, which is also known as ribwort or lance leaf plantain. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to help soothe and heal skin irritations. The active compounds in the Plantago lanceolata plant have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection in chapped lips. When applied topically, the creams can help to lock in moisture, calm the skin, and reduce the pain and discomfort associated with chapped lips. The natural ingredients in the creams are also gentle and non-irritating, making them suitable for people with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Plantago lanceolata herbal cream for chapped lips, first you need to make a plant infusion by boiling 100 grams of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves in 500 milliliters of water for 10-15 minutes. Let the mixture cool down and then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Next, you'll need to make a cream base by mixing 100 grams of beeswax, 100 grams of coconut oil, and 100 grams of shea butter in a saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture constantly until it's fully melted and smooth, then remove it from the heat. Now, add 20 milliliters of the Plantago lanceolata infusion to the cream base and stir well to combine. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean container, let it cool and solidify, and your Plantago lanceolata herbal cream is ready to use. Apply it to your chapped lips whenever you need to soothe and moisturize them.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago lanceolata herbal creams for chapped lips

intro_desc: Using herbal creams on chapped lips is a great way to get relief from dry, cracked skin. One of the main herbs used in these creams is Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera. This herb is amazing because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and redness in the affected area. It also has moisturizing properties, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. This makes aloe vera a perfect ingredient to include in lip balms and creams.  Another herb used in herbal creams is Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold. This herb has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause infections. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort in the affected area. Calendula is often used to treat wounds and skin irritations, making it a great addition to lip care products.  Chapped lips can be painful and uncomfortable, and if left untreated, they can become infected. Fixing the issue is important because it can improve your quality of life. Chapped lips can make it difficult to eat and speak, and can even affect your self-confidence. By using herbal creams on your lips, you can get relief from dry skin and prevent infections. This can help you to feel more confident and comfortable in your own skin, and can even improve your relationships with others.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal creams to remedy chapped lips is a blend of Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. Aloe barbadensis is a natural moisturizer that soothes and hydrates dry skin. It contains a compound called aloin that helps to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation. Calendula officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and protect the skin from further irritation. It also promotes wound healing and tissue repair, which is essential for repairing damaged lip tissue. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect the skin. It also helps to regulate the skin's natural pH balance, which is essential for preventing further dryness and irritation. This combination of herbs works synergistically to provide long-lasting hydration, soothe and calm the skin, and promote tissue repair.
supplementary_avoid: If you have chapped lips, it's best to avoid herbal creams with ingredients like turmeric, cayenne pepper, and wasabi. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which can be too harsh for dry skin and make chapped lips worse. Cayenne pepper and wasabi contain capsaicin, a compound that can irritate the skin and cause more inflammation, making chapped lips sore and tender.  Another ingredient to avoid is comfrey root's related herb: Euphorbia peplus. Euphorbia peplus contains a compound called euphorbin, which can be too irritating for sensitive skin. It can cause redness, itching, and stinging, making chapped lips worse.  Also, avoid creams with red clover (Trifolium pratense). Red clover can be too drying for the skin, especially on chapped lips. It can further dry out the skin and make it more sensitive.  Lastly, avoid creams with stinging nettle (Urtica dioica). Stinging nettle can cause skin irritation, itching, and redness, which can worsen chapped lips. It's best to stick with gentle and soothing ingredients that can help to heal and moisturize chapped lips.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help with chapped lips. It contains vitamins A, C, and E, which are good for skin health. When applied to the lips, aloe vera gel helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin. This is because aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and redness.  Coconut oil is another natural remedy that can help with chapped lips. It is rich in fatty acids that help to moisturize and protect the skin. When applied to the lips, coconut oil helps to create a barrier that prevents dry air from getting in and causing more irritation.  Beeswax is a natural wax that can help to lock in moisture and protect the lips from the environment. It is often used in lip balms and can be applied to the lips on its own. Beeswax helps to create a barrier that prevents dry air from getting in and causing more irritation.  Glycerin is a natural humectant that helps to retain moisture in the skin. When applied to the lips, glycerin helps to draw in moisture from the air and keep the skin hydrated. This is especially helpful for people who live in dry climates or have dry skin.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with chapped lips may also experience other health issues due to dryness and irritation in the mouth area. One common problem is mouth ulcers, which can be painful and make eating and speaking difficult. Aloe vera gel can help soothe and heal mouth ulcers due to its anti-inflammatory properties.  Another issue people with chapped lips may face is dry mouth, also known as xerostomia. This can lead to bad breath, difficulty swallowing, and an increased risk of tooth decay. Licorice root has been shown to stimulate saliva production and can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms.  People with chapped lips may also be more prone to dental problems like tooth decay and gum disease. This is because dry mouth creates an ideal environment for bacteria to thrive, leading to these issues. Turmeric, with its antibacterial properties, can help combat these problems.  Additionally, people with chapped lips may experience a sore throat, especially if they frequently lick their lips, which can introduce bacteria into the throat. Slippery elm can help soothe and protect the throat, reducing discomfort and inflammation.  Elderberry has also been used to help alleviate mouth and throat issues, including dry mouth, mouth ulcers, and sore throats. Its antiviral properties may also help boost the immune system, reducing the risk of infections.
